description: Ephemeral, read-only GitHub Actions agent that triages issues (classify + label) then dispatches an advisory persona (bug -> fix-it-man RCA, enhancement -> stoic-developer plan), powered by an OSS model via GitHub Models on the built-in GITHUB_TOKEN with zero external secrets. Hand-rolled stateless-model / stateful-harness agent loop in pure Python.

userStories.title: Scaffold package + label allow-list
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I need a fixed label vocabulary the agent applies from but never creates, so triage cannot invent labels.
acceptanceCriteria: triage/ package created with empty __init__.py and tests/__init__.py
requirements.txt pins requests==2.32.3 and pytest==8.3.3
labels.ALLOWED set + validate_labels() splits proposed labels into (accepted, rejected)
Off-list labels are rejected, never created; no area:* labels in allow-list
Tests pass (python -m pytest triage/tests/test_labels.py)

userStories.title: Verdict schema validation
userStories.description: As the pipeline, I need to validate the Stage-1 verdict so a malformed model output never reaches Stage-2.
acceptanceCriteria: schema.validate_verdict(v) returns [] when valid, else a list of error strings
Bad type/severity/route enums each produce an error; empty comment produces an error; non-list labels produce an error
Constants VALID_TYPES, VALID_SEVERITY, VALID_ROUTES exported
Tests pass (python -m pytest triage/tests/test_schema.py)

userStories.title: Read-only filesystem tools + schemas
userStories.description: As the triage agent, I need read_file/list_files/grep tools (truncated, hidden-dir-skipping) plus OpenAI tool schemas and a registry.
acceptanceCriteria: read_file truncates to MAX_OUTPUT; list_files skips hidden dirs; grep returns path:line: text
READONLY_SCHEMAS (OpenAI tool schemas) + READONLY_REGISTRY exported; finish_schema(properties) helper exported
READONLY_REGISTRY contains no run_command
Tests pass (python -m pytest triage/tests/test_tools.py)

userStories.title: run_command tool for fix-it-man (timeout-bounded)
userStories.description: As fix-it-man, I need a bounded run_command so I can run tests during RCA. Flagged: the only non-read-only capability in v1.
acceptanceCriteria: run_command(command, timeout=30) captures stdout+stderr, returns exit code, enforces timeout
FIXIT_SCHEMAS = READONLY_SCHEMAS + run_command; FIXIT_REGISTRY = read-only + run_command
Tests pass (python -m pytest triage/tests/test_tools.py, 7 total)

userStories.title: GitHub Models HTTP client with 429 backoff
userStories.description: As the harness, I need a thin raw-HTTP client for GitHub Models (OpenAI-compatible) with exponential 429 backoff.
acceptanceCriteria: call_model(messages, tools, *, max_retries=4, timeout=60) posts to API_URL with Bearer GITHUB_TOKEN + X-GitHub-Api-Version
Retries on HTTP 429 with exponential backoff; raises RuntimeError after retries exhausted
Constants API_URL, MODEL (env TRIAGE_MODEL), API_VERSION exported
Tests pass (python -m pytest triage/tests/test_llm.py)

userStories.title: Hand-rolled agent loop with defensive dispatch
userStories.description: As the system, I need run_agent() that re-sends full history each step (stateless model, stateful harness), with parse_tool_calls isolated and every dispatch branch recoverable.
acceptanceCriteria: run_agent(system_prompt, user_prompt, tools, registry, *, max_steps=12) returns the finish payload
parse_tool_calls(response) isolates the tool protocol; dispatch() returns (result_str, is_finish, payload) and never crashes on bad tool/args
Exceeding max_steps raises RuntimeError; unknown tool is recoverable
Tests pass (python -m pytest triage/tests/test_harness.py)

userStories.title: gh-CLI helpers for comments + labels
userStories.description: As Stage-1/Stage-2, I need write helpers (post_comment, add_labels, has_label) via the gh CLI.
acceptanceCriteria: add_labels builds a gh issue edit ... --add-label command; no-ops on empty labels
has_label parses gh issue view --json labels output to a bool
post_comment posts via gh issue comment
Tests pass (python -m pytest triage/tests/test_github_api.py)

userStories.title: Model-agnostic persona prompts (triage + 2 workers)
userStories.description: As each stage, I need a structured system prompt with a hard output contract: triage classifies+routes; fix-it-man does advisory RCA; stoic-developer does an advisory plan.
acceptanceCriteria: triage/personas/{triage,fix-it-man,stoic-developer}.md exist
triage prompt names finish, route, severity; worker prompts state the advisory boundary (do not write / do not modify)
Tests pass (python -m pytest triage/tests/test_personas.py)

userStories.title: Stage 1 entrypoint with idempotency + failure backstop
userStories.description: As the workflow, I need run_triage.main(): run the triage agent, validate the verdict, apply allow-listed labels, comment, write verdict.json, set route output. Idempotent; never silent on failure.
acceptanceCriteria: Happy path: applies accepted labels + 'triaged', drops off-list, comments, writes verdict.json, sets route output
Already-triaged is a no-op (route=none); failure adds needs-human-triage + fallback comment (route=none)
Persona loading resolves relative to the module file so it works from any CWD
Tests pass (python -m pytest triage/tests/test_run_triage.py)

userStories.title: Stage 2 advisory worker (route-dispatched persona)
userStories.description: As the workflow, I need run_worker.main(): load verdict.json, pick the persona by route, run it with the distilled Stage-1 handoff, post its analysis. Never silent on failure.
acceptanceCriteria: PERSONA_BY_ROUTE maps bug->fix-it-man(FIXIT tools), enhancement->stoic-developer(READONLY tools)
bug route runs fix-it-man and posts the RCA comment; failure posts a fallback comment
Full suite passes (python -m pytest triage/tests -v)

userStories.title: Idempotent label setup script
userStories.description: As the operator, I need a one-time idempotent script to pre-create the seven non-default labels in the repo.
acceptanceCriteria: triage/setup-labels.sh creates severity:*, regression, triaged, needs-human-triage via gh label create --force
Script is set -euo pipefail and re-runnable; reads REPO from env
Shell parses cleanly (bash -n triage/setup-labels.sh)

userStories.title: Two-job issue-triage workflow (author-gated, route-gated)
userStories.description: As the repo, I need .github/workflows/issue-triage.yml wiring env -> run_triage/run_worker, gating on author association + route, passing verdict.json via artifact, least-privilege permissions.
acceptanceCriteria: triage job author-gated (OWNER/MEMBER/COLLABORATOR), outputs route; worker job gated on route in (bug, enhancement)
permissions: models:read, issues:write, contents:read only; verdict.json passed via upload/download-artifact
Workflow YAML is valid

userStories.title: End-to-end verification runbook + top-level overview doc
userStories.description: As the author, I need a documented eval battery (bug/enhancement/question/idempotency/backstop/zero-secrets) and a high-level OVERVIEW so I can learn how the system is built and how it works.
acceptanceCriteria: OVERVIEW.md explains the architecture, the per-file map, and what each step does (learning-oriented)
A runbook documents the Task-13 live checks (cannot run in CI here; requires push + GitHub Models access)
Full suite still green (python -m pytest triage/tests -v)
